original_hypothesis.title: LockingMultiRewards recover() Can Drain Active Reward Token Balances
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LockingMultiRewards.recover() allows the owner to transfer any token except stakingToken (with excess-only protection for stakingToken). Reward tokens have NO protection - the owner can drain the contract's entire balance of any reward token, stealing pending and locked user rewards. The function does not account for undistributed or locked reward amounts.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The recover function only safeguards stakingToken via the stakingTokenBalance check. Reward tokens funded via notifyRewardAmount are held in the contract with no accounting protection against owner withdrawal.

proof_of_signal.pattern: recover() only checks stakingToken skim limit; no protection for reward tokens: tokenAddress.safeTransfer(owner, tokenAmount) with no rewardToken balance reservation

preconditions: Owner key compromise or malicious owner
Active reward distribution with non-zero reward token balance
Users have unclaimed rewards or locked reward tokens

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: recover(rewardToken, rewardToken.balanceOf(this)) would succeed regardless of pending reward obligations
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Owner calls recover for each reward token, draining all balances
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Owner extracts all reward tokens; users' getRewards() calls subsequently fail with insufficient balance

economic_questions: What is the typical reward token balance held in the contract?
How many users would be affected by reward token drainage?
Is the owner a multisig reducing single-point-of-failure risk?
validation_requirements: Check deployment script for ownership model

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Full reward token balance (all pending/locked rewards)

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All stakers with pending or locked rewards
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Sum of all reward token balances in contract

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Deployment owner, reward token balances, staker count
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: recover() followed by getRewards() failure test

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
